areas.label: The Economy
areas.synthesis: Remittances and industrial activity are both rebounding after a rough start to 2026, but extortion is emerging as a drag on small business growth — recovery and risk moving in parallel.

headlines.title: April economic activity (IGAE) rebounds +2.3% — strongest in ~5 years
La extorsión se alza como amenaza para el crecimiento de las pymes, advierte Concanaco

areas.label: Money & fintech
areas.synthesis: Moody's downgrade, rising coffee prices, and a Banxico rate outlook that stays flat this year before hikes in 2027 point to mounting cost pressure with little near-term policy relief.

headlines.title: Moody's cuts Mexico to Baa3, the last investment-grade rung
Precio del café se dispara en México; sube más de 100 pesos en un año
BNP Paribas prevé tres incrementos a la tasa de interés de Banxico para el 2027

areas.label: Politics & elections
areas.synthesis: Judicial elections are pushed to 2028 as the Court is restructured, while Sheinbaum reshuffles cabinet figures into party roles — institutions and personnel both realigning ahead of 2027.

headlines.title: Second judicial reform published in the DOF: judicial elections pushed to 2028, Supreme Court restructured
Sheinbaum confirms ~7 cabinet exits ahead of the 2027 elections

areas.label: Security
areas.synthesis: The government's homicide data, down roughly 46% since taking office and hitting a decade-low single day, remains the administration's central security claim.

headlines.title: Government reports homicides at multi-year lows — down ~46% since it took office

areas.label: US–Mexico
areas.synthesis: USMCA now faces annual renegotiation through 2036, a Section 122 tariff surcharge stays in force near its expiry, and Mexico is escalating with criminal complaints over migrant deaths — friction rising on trade and enforcement.

headlines.title: U.S. declines to renew USMCA, opening a 10-year annual-review cycle
Mexico to file U.S. criminal complaints over citizens' deaths in immigration enforcement
Court keeps the 10% Section 122 surcharge on non-USMCA Mexican goods in force — expires July 24
